methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Science Corporation is a Series B-stage biotech and health company founded by Max Hodak that has raised $200M+. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 private peers at the same stage and sector (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) alongside 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $380M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Science Corporation's revenue is not on file; the sector P/S median of 7.8x across the three public tickers cannot be deployed without disclosed revenue metrics.

The two applicable methods imply a valuation range of $323M (low) to $931M (high), with a median estimate of $595M. The 2.9x spread between methods reflects material dispersion in how peer biotech companies at this stage are valued. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue or revenue run-rate, clinical or technical milestones achieved, and the specific risk profile relative to the peer set. The peer median sits substantially above the stage-progression estimate, suggesting either above-average risk capital or differentiated technical capability warranting premium relative to pure stage dynamics.
